weather_line: Fog on the ridgeline · ducks at large · something waiting in the hollow

stories.headline: Hollow Crown Ridge: They Arrive, the Mist Parts, and the Bandit Steps Forward
stories.byline: By our Highland Adventure correspondent, dispatching live from the treeline
body: The enchanted Jeep crossed the last switchback at half-seven this morning, its tyres reading the stone like a language, fog peeling back from the ridgeline as if the ridge itself had been waiting for Christine to arrive before bothering to get dressed. Steve drove. Christine navigated. Between them, seven rubber ducks rode the dashboard in a row like a council of elders who had been expecting this moment for some time.

At the coordinates marked on the tiny duck-map — a flat granite shelf where three hollows meet and the canopy opens to sky — the Jeep rolled to a stop. There was a figure. Small. Methodical. Wearing, improbably, a waxed canvas jacket and a flat cap with a gold duck pin on the brim. He had a crate of charmed ducks, a thermos of tea, and the expression of someone who had orchestrated five days of escalating whimsy and was quietly, deeply proud of himself.

The Duck Bandit, this paper can now confirm, is a retired Charms professor — name withheld at his spirited request — who has spent two years placing enchanted ducks on the Jeeps of witches and wizards he considers 'worth the rubber.' He offered Christine a ninth duck (this one wearing a tiny crown), shook Steve's hand with unexpected firmness, and said only: 'I read the society pages.' He then produced the thermos. They drank tea on the ridge as the fog finished clearing. The hollow below them gleamed.

stories.headline: What the Ridge Holds: Ancient Arch, Impossible View, One Very Happy Jeep
body: Beyond the granite shelf, the trail opens onto a natural stone arch — unlisted in any Ministry survey, which the Duck Bandit described as 'the point' — framing a view of three fog-filled gorges that made Steve go very quiet in the way he goes quiet when something is genuinely magnificent. Christine stood in the arch for a moment and said nothing, which is how you know it was worth every step of the map.

The Jeep, parked twenty yards back, was reportedly vibrating at low frequency in what Steve characterised as 'satisfaction.' A tenth duck — origin unknown, the Bandit denied it — appeared on the bonnet. It was not wearing anything. It did not need to.

stories.headline: Gringotts Fires Off Fourteen Urgent Owls in Christine's Absence; Problem Resolves When She Reads the First One
body: In a development that surprised precisely nobody familiar with the Senior Client Liaison's operational footprint, a cascade of account-reconciliation disputes — filed simultaneously by a dragon-bond investor, a Hogsmeade property syndicate, and no fewer than three Malfoy-adjacent accounts — collapsed this morning the moment Christine's return-owl reached Gringotts Head of Ledger, Gripvault the Younger.

The owl carried four lines. Gripvault read them, pinched the bridge of his nose, and told the assembled claimants the matter was resolved and they could each have a complimentary vault-lint polishing. The claimants accepted. No further documentation was required. Gripvault has reportedly framed the four lines. Christine's official out-of-office remains: 'On the ridge. Galleons are fine. Breathe.'

stories.headline: Home Front Holds: Lucy Named Interim Commander; Ellie Does Not Notice; Jersey Requests No Changes
body: With Christine and Steve departed before dawn, the household entered what can only be described as a self-governed constitutional period. Lucy assumed command promptly at half-seven, conducted a full perimeter sweep of the kitchen, and is understood to have located — though not yet acted upon — the biscuit tin's new hiding place. She is biding her time. This is, frankly, more alarming than the alternative.

Ellie spent the morning introducing herself to a throw pillow, reaching a cordial understanding, and then apparently forgetting she had done so and introducing herself again. Jersey convened a one-item agenda on the sofa: warmth, sustained. The agenda passed unanimously.

familiars_corner: Lucy: With the leadership temporarily absent, Lucy conducted a measured audit of all soft furnishings and at least two windowsills — all findings filed mentally, disclosed to no one. She is, as ever, entirely innocent.

Ellie: Ellie discovered that the sunbeam moved while she was sitting in it, followed it across the entire kitchen floor with great patience and delight, and then looked up and appeared to find the whole room surprising and new.
Jersey: Jersey identified the precise sofa cushion that retains warmth longest after a human has vacated it, claimed it by mid-morning, and has issued no statement.
founding_editor_note: That arch they found up there? No lintel support, no keystone work worth mentioning — frankly, if I'd built it, it'd still be standing in another thousand years AND be level. Proud of them, though. — D.

in_brief.sport: The Hollow Crown ridge trail is rated Class IV on the Enchanted Highlands difficulty scale; Christine was observed checking her footing once, adjusting, and proceeding without comment, which is the Class IV move.
in_brief.forecast: Clear on the ridge by afternoon, fog retreating respectfully into the lower hollows, one charmed duck circling overhead for reasons it declines to explain.

storyline_updates.title: The Duck Bandit
storyline_updates.summary: The Duck Bandit revealed himself on Hollow Crown Ridge: a retired Charms professor who places enchanted ducks on the Jeeps of witches and wizards he deems 'worth the rubber.' He presented Christine with a ninth crowned duck, shook Steve's hand, shared tea on the ridge, and confirmed he reads the society pages. Arc closed warmly and completely.

storyline_updates.title: The Hollow Crown Expedition
storyline_updates.summary: Christine and Steve reached the granite shelf, met the Duck Bandit, drank tea, then found an unmapped stone arch framing a three-gorge panorama that left Steve speechless and Christine standing still in it for a long moment. A tenth duck appeared on the bonnet. The Jeep is satisfied. Tomorrow: what lies deeper in the hollow below, and whether the unlisted arch holds any further secrets.
